source_jobs.title: Grant Writer (Europe)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT CFS
Climate Finance Solutions (CFS) is a global consultancy that helps companies and organizations secure large-scale grant funding for climate solutions. To date, our team has secured over $1.6 billion in non-dilutive funding for clients across Europe, the US, Canada, Australia, and emerging markets worldwide. As a mission-driven company, we are committed to providing equitable access to the climate funding ecosystem and building the next generation of leaders in climate finance.

ABOUT THE ROLE
As a Grant Writer at CFS, you’ll do more than write proposals. You’ll partner with climate innovators to develop competitive applications for EU and national grant funding, advising clients on project design and commercialization plans, while shaping partnership strategies that materially improve their chances of getting funded.

We’re a fast-paced, fully remote team. Contractors who deliver consistently are considered for our Grant Development Lead role.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
• Work with senior staff, the Director of Grants, other grant writers, and the administrative team to bring high-quality proposals through the entire development process: project design and development, document preparation, and submission
• Support multiple proposal projects simultaneously, maintaining quality and meeting tight deadlines
• Draft complex technical narratives, work plans, budget justifications, letters of commitment, and other key elements of EU, national, and other public funding programmes
• Conduct external research, including from peer-reviewed sources, and gather internal data to provide content for proposals
• Serve as a strategic advisor to client teams, refining project scope, commercialization plans, business development approaches, fundraising strategy, and organizational positioning before drafting begins, so proposals reflect a compelling funding strategy rather than just compliant documentation
• Liaise with external partners and stakeholders to support proposal development and consortium building

QUALIFICATIONS
Required
• Bachelor’s degree (or international equivalent)
• Fluency in English
• Minimum of six years of grant writing experience; at least three years as the lead writer on funded grant proposals
• Strong scientific or technical knowledge relevant to climate solutions
• Experience securing grants from EU funding agencies, including, but not limited to, Horizon Europe, the European Innovation Council (EIC), the EU Innovation Fund, and the LIFE Programme
• Clear and compelling writing style, with outstanding grammar and research skills
• Strong oral and written communication skills for working cooperatively with internal teams, clients, and external partners
• Proficiency in Google Workspace, Microsoft Office, and other standard writing and collaborative content development platforms
• Ability to maintain project schedules and deadlines
• Self-motivated and able to work independently in a fast-paced startup environment
• Equipment, technical ability, flexibility, and temperament required to work fully remotely with intercontinental teams and clients, including weekday availability to check and respond to project communications
• Mission-driven, with a clear understanding of the urgency of addressing the climate crisis

Preferred
• Graduate degree, particularly in a scientific or technical field
• Experience as an evaluator for EU or national funding programmes, in particular the EU Innovation Fund and Horizon Europe
• Multi-lingual writing capability and extensive experience with national funding agencies
• Knowledge of climate sectors, including renewable energy, carbon management, energy storage/batteries, industrial decarbonization, critical minerals and materials, hydrogen and other clean fuels, grid technologies, transportation, sustainable agriculture, advanced manufacturing, nuclear, and more
• Pre-award research administration, grant management, or project management certification (e.g., PMP, PRINCE2)

OUR COMM

source_jobs.title: Full Stack Software Engineer (CFS Platform)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT CFS
Climate Finance Solutions (CFS) is a global consultancy that helps companies and organizations secure large-scale grant funding to develop and scale their high-impact climate solutions. The CFS team has a strong track record of success, having secured over $1.6 billion in grant funding. CFS is mission-driven, aiming to provide equitable access to the climate funding ecosystem. It is focused on supporting high-impact, climate-related ventures, and training and growing high-potential hires to develop the next class of leaders in climate.

SUMMARY
The CFS Platform (internally known as GMS) is the technology layer underneath our funding work. It combines an internal workflow tool used by our funding team, a secure client portal, AI-assisted analysis of solicitations and proposal documents, and a growing dataset of funding opportunities and program intelligence built up over years of practice. Today, much of this work lives across Airtable, Google Drive, email, and Slack. We are turning that into a real product so our team can move faster across more opportunities, our clients have a better experience throughout the funding process, and CFS can open up new lines of business in funding intelligence and self-serve tooling.

This is the first dedicated software engineering hire on the platform. You will work directly with the founder and a small group of collaborators, with real ownership over what gets built and how. The codebase is early enough that you can shape it, and the product is real enough that what you ship gets used the same week by our funding team and our clients. The role is a strong fit for someone who wants to build a serious product without the layers and process of a larger company, and who finds climate and industrial technology genuinely interesting.

Beyond technical skills, this role is a good fit for someone with genuine interest in the climate work CFS does and the drive to help build a product that supports it. The right candidate is self-directed, able to execute on day-to-day implementation while also helping shape the platform as it matures.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Build and ship product features across the frontend and backend

 - Improve the internal workflow tool and the client-facing portal

 - Contribute to AI-assisted features for solicitation analysis, document workflows, and drafting support

 - Fix bugs and improve reliability as usage grows

 - Work closely with the founder and other collaborators to turn ideas into shipped functionality

 - Support related automation work across the company’s operational and business development functions


QUALIFICATIONS

 - Strong proficiency with AI coding tools (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Copilot, and similar) as a daily part of your workflow, with the judgment to review and refine what they produce rather than ship it as-is

 - Solid experience building and shipping production web applications

 - Strong TypeScript and React skills, with comfort working across the stack

 - Good product instincts and the ability to take a feature from rough idea to working implementation without heavy oversight

 - Care for code quality alongside speed of delivery

 - Must be able to work independently and be self-motivated, while thriving in a startup company culture and fast-paced work environment


NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience with Supabase or Postgres

 - Experience shipping AI-assisted product features in production

 - Experience with document-heavy workflows, internal tools, or B2B SaaS products

 - Background in or genuine curiosity about climate, energy, or industrial technology


OUR COMMITMENT TO INCLUSIVITY
Climate Finance Solutions values diversity and is committed to fostering an equitable and inclusive environment. We firmly believe that a broad range of perspectives, backgrounds, and experiences is essential for addressing the complex challenges of climate change. We promote climate and social justice through our climate projects and within our o

source_jobs.title: Grants Project Manager (Europe)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT CFS
Climate Finance Solutions (CFS) is a global consultancy that helps companies and organizations secure large-scale grant funding for climate solutions. To date, our team has secured over $1.6 billion in non-dilutive funding for clients across Europe, the US, Canada, Australia, and emerging markets worldwide. As a mission-driven company, we are committed to providing equitable access to the climate funding ecosystem and building the next generation of leaders in climate finance.

ABOUT THE ROLE
As a Grants Project Manager, Europe at CFS, you’ll own EU and national grant applications end-to-end, leading writing teams and managing complex multi-stakeholder timelines, while serving as the strategic interface between CFS and our clients. You’ll bring rigor and momentum to high-stakes proposals from kickoff through submission, with quality and competitive positioning as the priority.

We’re a fast-paced, fully remote team. Contractors who deliver consistently are considered for our Senior Project Manager role.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
• Manage grant applications from client kickoff through submission, ensuring compliance with all requirements and deadlines
• Develop project timelines, milestones, and go/no-go decision points aligned with solicitation requirements, and create project tasks assigned to the appropriate team members
• Serve as the primary point of contact between CFS and the client/partners, providing weekly status updates on project status and pending action items
• Coordinate writers and technical experts across proposal teams, balancing expertise with capacity to avoid burnout
• Oversee compliance with EU grant regulations and the EU Grant Agreement framework; manage submissions across the EU Funding and Tenders Portal and applicable national portals
• Serve as a strategic advisor to client teams on competitive positioning and partnership development, helping shape applications to maximize funding outcomes
• Coordinate cross-functionally between CFS team members and external partners to ensure smooth proposal development and submission

QUALIFICATIONS
Required
• Bachelor’s degree (or international equivalent)
• Fluency in English
• Minimum of six years of grant project management or grant writing experience; at least three years as lead project manager on funded grant proposals
• Strong scientific or technical knowledge relevant to climate solutions
• Experience managing applications to EU funding agencies, including, but not limited to, Horizon Europe, the European Innovation Council (EIC), the EU Innovation Fund, and the LIFE Programme
• Strong knowledge of EU grant compliance, including Horizon Europe Programme Rules, the EU Grant Agreement framework, and applicable State Aid requirements
• Working knowledge of the EU Funding and Tenders Portal; national portal experience is a plus
• Proficiency in Asana, Google Workspace, Microsoft Office, and other standard project management and collaborative platforms
• Strong oral and written communication skills for working cooperatively with internal teams, clients, and external partners
• Detail-oriented with strong analytical and problem-solving abilities
• Exceptional organizational and time management skills, with the ability to work independently in a fast-paced startup environment
• Equipment, technical ability, flexibility, and temperament required to work fully remotely with intercontinental teams and clients, including weekday availability to check and respond to project communications
• Mission-driven, with a clear understanding of the urgency of addressing the climate crisis

Preferred
• Graduate degree, particularly in a related field (business administration, public administration, or a scientific or technical field)
• Prior grant writing experience
• Experience as an evaluator for EU or national funding programmes, in particular the EU Innovation Fund and Horizon Europe
• Multi-lingual writing capability and extensive experience with national funding age

source_jobs.title: Grants Project Manager (United States)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT CFS
Climate Finance Solutions (CFS) is a global consultancy that helps companies and organizations secure large-scale grant funding for climate solutions. To date, our team has secured over $1.6 billion in non-dilutive funding for clients across the US, Europe, Canada, Australia, and emerging markets worldwide. As a mission-driven company, we are committed to providing equitable access to the climate funding ecosystem and building the next generation of leaders in climate finance.

ABOUT THE ROLE
As a Grants Project Manager, US at CFS, you’ll own federal and state grant applications end-to-end, leading writing teams and managing complex multi-stakeholder timelines, while serving as the strategic interface between CFS and our clients. You’ll bring rigor and momentum to high-stakes proposals from kickoff through submission, with quality and competitive positioning as the priority.

We’re a fast-paced, fully remote team. Contractors who deliver consistently are considered for our Senior Project Manager role.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
• Manage grant applications from client kickoff through submission, ensuring compliance with all requirements and deadlines
• Develop project timelines, milestones, and go/no-go decision points aligned with solicitation requirements, and create project tasks assigned to the appropriate team members
• Serve as the primary point of contact between CFS and the client/partners, providing weekly status updates on project status and pending action items
• Coordinate writers and technical experts across proposal teams, balancing expertise with capacity to avoid burnout
• Oversee compliance with federal grant regulations (2 CFR 200) and budget and standard forms (SF424 family); manage submissions across federal platforms (SAM.gov http://SAM.gov, Grants.gov http://Grants.gov Workspace, DOE eXCHANGE) and relevant state platforms
• Serve as a strategic advisor to client teams on competitive positioning and partnership development, helping shape applications to maximize funding outcomes
• Coordinate cross-functionally between CFS team members and external partners to ensure smooth proposal development and submission

QUALIFICATIONS
Required
• Bachelor’s degree (or international equivalent)
• Fluency in English
• Minimum of six years of grant project management or grant writing experience; at least three years as lead project manager on funded grant proposals
• Strong scientific or technical knowledge relevant to climate solutions
• Experience managing applications to climate-focused federal and state agencies, including, but not limited to, the US Department of Energy (DOE), US Department of Agriculture (USDA), Department of Defense/War (DOD/DOW), National Science Foundation (NSF), Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), California Energy Commission (CEC), New York State Energy Research and Development Authority (NYSERDA), and MassCEC
• Strong knowledge of federal grant compliance and regulations (2 CFR 200) and federal grant budgets and standard forms (SF424 family)
• Working knowledge of federal grant submission platforms, including SAM.gov http://SAM.gov, Grants.gov http://Grants.gov Workspace, and DOE eXCHANGE; state platform experience is a plus
• Proficiency in Asana, Google Workspace, Microsoft Office, and other standard project management and collaborative platforms
• Strong oral and written communication skills for working cooperatively with internal teams, clients, and external partners
• Detail-oriented with strong analytical and problem-solving abilities
• Exceptional organizational and time management skills, with the ability to work independently in a fast-paced startup environment
• Equipment, technical ability, flexibility, and temperament required to work fully remotely with intercontinental teams and clients, including weekday availability to check and respond to project communications
• Mission-driven, with a clear understanding of the urgency of addressing the climate crisis

Preferred
• Gra
